title: Heatwave Continues Across Andhra Pradesh and Telangana - The Hans India
full_text:
Heatwave Continues Across Andhra Pradesh and Telangana
Andhra Pradesh and Telangana are currently enduring intense heat and heatwaves, with residents struggling to cope with scorching temperatures, hot winds, and high humidity. The Meteorological Department has issued a fresh heatwave alert for Andhra Pradesh, predicting maximum temperatures between 42 and 44 degrees Celsius in several areas of the Coastal and Rayalaseema regions. The department estimates that severe heatwaves will affect 46 mandals across the state, while 73 mandals are expected to experience regular heatwaves. Authorities have urged residents to remain vigilant and avoid unnecessary outdoor activities.
Heatwave Alert and District-Specific Warnings
The Andhra Pradesh Disaster Management Authority reported that severe heatwave conditions are expected to prevail today in four mandals of Srikakulam, 19 in Vizianagaram, 14 in Parvathipuram Manyam, and smaller numbers in other districts, including Alluri Sitarama Raju, Polavaram, Anakapalli, Kakinada, and East Godavari. Warnings extend to 73 additional mandals across the state. Temperatures soared to 45°C in Ontimitta (Kadapa district), with other districts recording highs of around 43.8°C in Varadayyapalem (Tirupati) and 43°C in Anumalapalli (Prakasam). Overall, 262 mandals across Andhra Pradesh have experienced temperatures of 45°C or higher. Officials have advised residents to take precautions against the oppressive heat, which is expected to persist until April 18.
Telangana Faces Unbearable Heat
Meanwhile, Telangana is experiencing similarly extreme conditions, with maximum temperatures frequently exceeding 43°C. The Hyderabad Meteorological Department warned that temperatures could surpass 45°C over the next three days. On Tuesday, the highest temperature recorded was 43.5°C in Adividevulapalli (Nalgonda district). Other districts, including Nizamabad, Adilabad, Siddipet, Kamareddy, Karimnagar, and Mancherial, recorded temperatures between 42.8°C and 43.3°C. The intense heat begins as early as 8:00 AM and lasts until 4:00 PM, prompting officials to advise the public to avoid outdoor activities between 11:00 AM and 3:00 PM and to wear protective clothing, such as hats or umbrellas.
